task_updates.executor_notes: Added the requested explanatory comments, replaced the two gallery shot handler wrappers with direct function references after verifying the model uses the same MediaGallery handler type, and removed the unused shot-creation aliases after repo-wide grep showed no imports outside the target file.
Simplified hasDataTransferType to the requested includes-first plus indexed-fallback shape while preserving the null/undefined guard for external drags. The dragDrop test file passed unchanged.

Extracted the portal/menu JSX into ClipContextMenu in the same file, kept the portal target at document.body, threaded all menu state through explicit props, and kept the file at 306 lines. ClipAction.test.tsx passed after the extraction.

Added a ref-based field-by-field bailout over all modifier flags, kept the initial render state from useState(EMPTY_STATE), and avoided any ref/state feedback loop. The hook test file passed after the change.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. The AgentChat and shot-creation comments match the current logic, the GenerationsPaneGallery replacement is a true pass-through because the gallery model uses the same MediaGallery handler signature, and repo-wide grep found no remaining uses of ShotGenerationPreview or ShotGenerationRow in shotCreationTypes.
Yes. hasDataTransferType still returns false when dataTransfer.types is missing, and the implementation now keeps exactly two paths: types.includes() first, then manual indexed iteration.
Yes. ClipContextMenu gets its state and callbacks explicitly via props, still renders the same portal into document.body, and the file is 306 lines.

Yes. The bailout compares shiftKey, metaKey, ctrlKey, and isMultiSelectModifier individually, preserves the initial mount state through useState, and only mutates a ref before setState so it cannot loop.
